sanskrit:
दूरेण ह्यवरं कर्म बुद्धियोगाद्धनञ्जय।
बुद्धौ शरणमन्विच्छ कृपणाः फलहेतवः॥
transliteration:
dūreṇa hy-avaraṃ karma buddhi-yogād dhanañ-jaya |
buddhau śaraṇam anviccha kṛpaṇāḥ phala-hetavaḥ ||
literal_meaning: Far away indeed [discard] the inferior action / through the yoga of intelligence, O Conqueror of Wealth; / take refuge in [right] intelligence / wretched are the fruit-seekers.
contextual_meaning: Krishna urges Arjuna to drop the anxious calculus of outcomes and anchor his mind in a higher-order purpose (buddhi-yoga). Clinging to personal gain is framed as a small-hearted, almost compulsive, avoidance of existential responsibility.
psychological_state: Pre-decision paralysis dominated by outcome-oriented rumination; ego threatened by potential failure and moral ambiguity.
trigger_scenario: A mid-career professional offered a promotion that requires ethically gray decisions, obsessively calculating bonuses versus quietly choosing the role that serves team wellbeing.
anchor_text: Act from clear purpose, not for personal gain.
